commit.message:
fix(email): auto-reconnect dead IMAP connections + improve contact name resolution

IMAP connections were dying during long Telegram sync cycles (7+ min),
causing all email syncs to silently fail with "Connection not available".
Added reconnect-on-failure logic inside syncAccount that detects dead
connections and re-establishes them before retrying.

Also fixed contact dedup: previously, if the first email mentioning an
address had no display name (e.g., in a To: header), that contact was
permanently unnamed. Now re-yields contacts when a name is available,
letting the DB upsert fill in the display_name via COALESCE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Route LLM API key resolution through credential vault

Primary, fallback, and auto-detected LLM provider API keys now resolve
through the CredentialResolver chain (vault → dotenv → env var) instead
of raw std::env::var(). This means API keys stored in the encrypted
vault at ~/.openfang/vault.enc are automatically used for LLM drivers.

(public access) so the analytics tab loads without Yandex login.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Persist startCommit in database to fix commit detection race condition

The previous fix still had a race condition because startingCommitRef was
populated via an async fetch that could complete after the first agent
execution finished.

Now we:
1. Capture HEAD commit hash when sandbox is created (server-side)
2. Store it in Branch.startCommit in the database
3. Use branch.startCommit directly in the frontend (no async fetch needed)

This eliminates the race condition entirely because the baseline commit
is known before any agent execution can start.

For existing branches without startCommit, we fall back to setting the
baseline on first commit detection (skipping that detection cycle).

feat: {{variable}} template interpolation in TextBlocks * feat: {{variable}} template interpolation in TextBlocks Post-evaluation pass resolves {{var}} tags in markdown TextBlocks against the final environment with display formatting. Enables forward references — summary tables at document top can show results computed below. Supports all output paths (md/html/json/text/TUI preview/doceval), preserves raw {{var}} tags in .cm files and TUI editing, applies scale/convert_to transforms, and handles whitespace-tolerant syntax. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * docs: template interpolation in language reference, user guide, and Services P&L Add {{variable}} to the feature registry, a Template Interpolation section in the language reference and user guide, and an "At a Glance" summary table to the Services P&L example demonstrating forward references. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * feat: wrap interpolated values in for HTML output Uses STX/ETX sentinel characters during interpolation that survive markdown rendering, then post-processes into tags. The default HTML template styles interpolated values with the calc-result blue and a subtle underline highlight. Plain text, markdown, JSON, and TUI output paths are unaffected — they continue to use the plain interpolated source. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * feat: bold-wrap interpolated values in markdown, strip backticks from {{var}} tags Interpolation regex now consumes optional surrounding backticks so `{{var}}` resolves to **value** instead of `value`. This prevents wrapping in HTML and gives bold emphasis in markdown output. - Markdown/text output: **value** (bold) - HTML output: value (unchanged) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: replace gradient background with border-bottom for .cm-interpolated The linear-gradient background created an unwanted gray/blue area over interpolated values. Switch to a subtle bottom border instead. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: simplify .cm-interpolated to bold only, no color or decoration Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * docs: add {{variable}} interpolation to all worked examples and expand docs Add interpolated prose summaries to 6 example .cm files (household-budget, job-offer, datacenter-cost, system-sizing, project-workback, recipe-scaling). Expand language reference and user guide with forward references, inline formatting, and table interpolation sections. Update all example index pages to list template interpolation in their features. Fix lesson-31: remove extra that was prematurely closing scroll-container Root cause of the 3-column layout (sections 1-3 at 15%, section 4 at 45%, nav at 30%): An earlier patch inserted to close both week-planner and planner-area at once, but left the original planner-area in place. This redundant closing div cascaded through the file: it closed activity-card too early, then the next two s closed section-3 and the scroll-container, leaving section 4 and btn-container as bare flex items in — which has display:flex — producing the horizontal column layout visible on ALL screen sizes including desktop/Live Server. Fix: remove the single extra (line 254 in previous version) between subject-grid's closing tag and the "Academic Encouragement" activity-title. Now the closing chain is correct: → closes week-planner + planner-area → closes subject-grid → closes activity-card → closes section 3 (section 4 + btn-container remain inside scroll-container) Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix preview pane showing (no output) for all agents (#350)

The performance PR (ea10e26) added num_lines to PollingStatusDetector
but not to StatusDetectorDispatcher or HookStatusDetector. Every TUI
status update threw a silent TypeError caught by the blanket except,
returning empty content for all agents.

Propagate num_lines through the full detect_status chain:
protocol → dispatcher → hook detector → polling fallback.

Also disable tmux automatic-rename on new windows to prevent tmux
from renaming agent windows based on the running process, which
would break name-based window lookups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auth: handle code exchange in middleware with proper cookie transfer

The previous approach lost session cookies because:
1. NextResponse.redirect() with { headers } doesn't transfer Set-Cookie
2. The callback route's collected cookies weren't synced with getAll()

Now middleware exchanges the code, then copies all auth cookies from
supabaseResponse to the redirect response using the cookies API.
Callback route is now just a fallback redirect.

commit.message:
fix: show email instead of generic "User" when name is missing (#180)

Invited users displayed "User" as their name because Clerk may not have
their name set during initial signup. Now the dashboard and sidebar fall
back to the user's email when the name is unavailable. Additionally, the
backend re-syncs profile data from Clerk when a user's first_name is
missing, fixing stale data in member lists.

Closes #141

[DOCS] Add dim_season redesign decision summary for CEO

7 key decisions requiring approval:
1. UUID primary key (vs VARCHAR)
2. JSONB rules storage (vs separate columns, vs hybrid)
3. Data validation location (database, application, both)
4. Mid-season rule changes (simple vs versioned)
5. RLS enablement timing (launch vs Phase 2)
6. Index strategy (minimal vs complete vs phased)
7. Migration timeline (3-phase approach)

Decision matrix provided for quick CEO review.
Related: DIM_SEASON_REDESIGN_REVIEW.md (full technical detail)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mobile chat view scrolling

- Add overflow-hidden to mobile chat container
- Add shrink-0 to mobile header to prevent shrinking
- Wrap ChatPanel in flex container with min-h-0 and overflow-hidden
- Add overflow-hidden to ChatPanel root div
- Add shrink-0 to header and input sections
- Add min-h-0 to messages container for proper flex behavior

The key fix is ensuring the flex container hierarchy properly
constrains heights so overflow-y-auto can work on the messages div.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix calendar agenda mixing events from previous day due to timezone bug

Graph API interprets bare datetimes as UTC, so Bogota midnight boundaries
were off by 5 hours, including previous evening events and missing current
evening events. Now converts Bogota boundaries to UTC before querying.

Fix per-structure material detection in multi-structure claims (E078)

3-tier override hierarchy: per-struct estimate_request > classifiable
shingle_type voting > claim-wide fallback. Prevents claim-wide dropdown
from forcing all structures to same material. Added "flat" to material_labels.
3 new tests: override_conflict, per_structure_override, no_shingle_type_fallback.

Coverage, Memory subsystems and Signal Trigger Engine.

- Compares encoder weights against fresh initialization (L2 norm <1%)
- Prevents silent garbage predictions from random GNN weights

Apply manuscript patches: fix monotonicity theorem, stabilization language, NML terminology

- Theorem 2: add velocity-matching condition with counterexample remark
- Theorem 3: "consistency" → "stabilization", add RL convergence remark
- New Theorem 4 (Nonidentifiability): promoted from remark to formal theorem
- Old Theorem 4 → Theorem 5 (Identifiability under Aperiodic Residuals)
- "exact NML" → "asymptotic NML" / "Bernoulli NML" throughout
- "converges" → "stabilizes" for period selection language
- "defects" → "projection residuals" in formal contexts
- "validation" → "experiments", "confirmed" → "consistent with"
- Add Limitation 0: velocity-matching requirement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix conda upload: try without --force first, fallback to --force

The --force flag causes prefix.dev to return 500 "Package version does
not exist" on first upload of a new version. Upload without --force
first (works for new versions), then retry with --force (works for
re-uploads of existing versions).

commit.message:
feat: improve Cmax prediction — AAFE 3.18 → 2.61 (passes <3.0)

- Add peff floor (0.5e-4 cm/s) for oral drugs with active transport
- Use RDKit Crippen logP for Kp instead of ML logP (more accurate)
- Detect compound type (base/acid/zwitterion) via SMARTS for Kp ionization
- Base-specific Berezhkovskiy alpha for lysosomal trapping correction
- Basic amine renal CL via OCT2 secretion detection

AUC AAFE: 2.20 → 1.74. Cmax AAFE: 3.18 → 2.61. Both pass <3.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse GPS data from Fitbit TCX files in Activities directory

GPS data in Fitbit Google Takeout is stored in separate .tcx files in the
Activities/ directory, not embedded in exercise JSON files. The parser was
only reading JSON files so gps_data was always None, producing TCX output
with no coordinates.

Changes:
- Add _parse_tcx_gps_points(): parses latitude, longitude, altitude,
  distance, time, and heart_rate from a Fitbit TCX file using Python's
  xml.etree.ElementTree; handles both namespaced and bare element names;
  correctly uses 'is not None' checks (leaf Elements are falsy in Python)
- Add _attach_gps_from_tcx_files(): scans Activities/ for .tcx files and
  attaches parsed GPS points to matching activities using two strategies:
  1. logId match when the TCX filename stem is a number
  2. Start-time match (±60 s tolerance) after parsing the <Id> element
- Call _attach_gps_from_tcx_files() from _parse_activities() after all
  JSON activity metadata has been loaded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Apple 2.1(b) IAP rejection, wake word detection, and security hardening

- Fix purchase race condition: wait for server DB write before resolving
  success to UI (root cause of Apple rejection on iPad Pro M4)
- Add retry on server verification failure
- Handle "already owned" error by auto-restoring instead of dead-end
- Reduce iPad IAP startup delay from 5.5s to 2.5s
- Lower wake word threshold from 0.9999 to 0.9 for real-world detection
- Add error boundary on main screen to prevent white-screen crashes
- Add guest guards on profile, family, and settings screens
- Clean up Mira audio files after processing (privacy + storage)
- Clear all stores and realtime channels on sign-out (prevent data leaks)
- Guard against out-of-order Apple notifications overwriting active subs
- Add server-side quota enforcement in increment_daily_usage RPC
- Fix timezone bug: use local date for daily quota reset
- Add Apple App Store Server API transaction validation in verify-receipt
- Deploy Supabase secrets for Apple IAP API keys

commit.message:
R15: 5 Cell programs designed and eval-one executed as pure Cell frames (ce-vslr)

5 programs of increasing complexity, 24 cells, 22 eval-one steps:
- p1: parallel confluence (3 independent cells, 6 valid orderings)
- p2: spawner cascade (⊢⊢ generates 3 answer cells dynamically)
- p3: quotation mirror (§ reads/modifies cell definitions as data)
- p4: bottom cascade (⊥ production, 3 handler strategies, retry)
- p5: self-describing (program reads own structure via §, predicts execution)

All output is pure Cell. Observations encoded as ⊢= cells.
Key findings: eval-one works, confluence holds, § is powerful,
spawner shape needs formalization, error-value semantics ambiguous.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
